rulings.Case Title: FORT BRAGG LOCAL BUSINESS MATTERS, AN VS. CALIFORNIA COASTAL COMMISSION
rulings.Calendar Matter: HEARING ON PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Tuesday, December 17, 2024, Line 1, HEARING ON PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE. Transferred to be heard in Department 502 on January 13, 2025, at 1:30 p.m. Judge Ross, who will be presiding over CEQA matters pursuant to the new judicial assignments for 2025, will hold a CMC at this time. Contact the CEQA Clerk at 415-551-3703 with further questions. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: BANK OF AMERICA, N.A. VS. MANUEL VANEGAS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of And Motion For Order Setting Aside Judgment And Dismissing Entire Action With Prejudice
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Tuesday, December 17, 2024, Line 3, PLAINTIFF BANK OF AMERICA, N.A.'s Motion For Order Setting Aside Judgment And Dismissing Entire Action With Prejudice. Plaintiff Bank of America, N.A.'s Motion For Order Setting Aside Judgment And Dismissing Entire Action With Prejudice, which is unopposed, is GRANTED. The judgment entered May 31, 2019, is vacated and set aside. The matter is dismissed with prejudice. Plaintiff must file a proposed order. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: CREDIT CORP SOLUTIONS INC., VS. JASON JONES ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Tuesday, December 17, 2024, Line 7, CROSS-DEFENDANT CREDIT CORP SOLUTIONS INC.'S DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT. Plaintiff and Cross-Defendant Credit Corp. Solutions Inc.'s demurrer to Defendant and Cross-Complainant Jason Jones' Cross-Complaint to which no opposition was filed is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. Neither the first nor the second cause of action constitutes a claim upon which relief can be granted, especially if the allegations regarding Plaintiff/Cross-Defendant litigation conduct are removed as bases for liability, as they must be pursuant to the litigation privilege. (Civ. Code, Sec. 47, subd. (b)(2) "A privileged publication or broadcast is one made [] (b) In any[] (2) judicial proceeding."]; Silberg v. Anderson (1990) 50 Cal.3d 205, 212 The usual formulation is that the privilege applies to any communication (1) made in judicial or quasi-judicial proceedings; (2) by litigants or other participants authorized by law; (3) to achieve the objects of the litigation; and (4) that have some connection or logical relation to the action.]; Gallegos v. Pac. Lumber Co. (2008) 158 Cal.App.4th 950, 958, citing Silberg , supra, 50 Cal.3d at 212 [The litigation privilege "renders absolutely privileged communications made as part of a 'judicial or quasi-judicial proceeding."'].) Intervenor is granted 20 days leave to amend from the time of notice of the order on this demurrer. Plaintiff and Cross-Defendant Credit Corp. Solutions Inc.'s purported motion to strike is not properly before the court and, thus, ordered off calendar. A motion to strike must be separately noticed and argued. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: BLESSINGS MAKANJE VS. BAY BRIDGE TOWING ASSISTANCE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Tuesday, December 17, 2024, Line 8, MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD AS TO PLAINTIFF BLESSINGS MAKANJE. Plaintiff's counsel Nathan Samooha's Motion to be Relieved as Counsel to which no opposition has been received is before the court. APPEARANCE REQUIRED. Moving counsel should be prepared to: (1) identify and discuss the facts informing the legal conclusion set forth in section 2 of his declaration in support of the motion; and, (2) explain why counsel of record Daniel D.
